lines: Five days. Ten envelopes. A story written for you alone.
Each morning and each evening, a sealed letter arrives.
Your choices shape every word of it.
Read when I send. Answer honestly. Do completely.
I am always watching. I already know which one you'll reach for.

prologue.label: Day One · Morning
prologue.intro: You wake up. The apartment is quiet. You go to the kitchen and find something waiting on the counter—flowers, fresh cut, in a glass vase you don't remember owning. Beside it, a cream-coloured envelope with your name written across it in familiar handwriting.

choices.title: The Flowers
choices.hint: You notice them first. You always do.

card.heading: Take them. Touch the petals.
card.body: You pick up the vase. The flowers are real—expensive ones, the kind that don't come from the grocery store. You touch one petal and it's cool, slightly damp. There's a note underneath the vase on the counter.
card.rule: Open the envelope now.

choices.title: The Envelope
choices.hint: You know what it means.

card.heading: Don't touch the flowers yet.
card.body: You reach past the vase for the envelope instead. Your hand is steady. Your pulse is not. The envelope is heavier than it looks. Inside, you already know what you'll find—a letter written before dawn, for you to read now, while the kitchen is still dark enough to feel private.
card.rule: Open the envelope.

morning.label: Day One · Morning
morning.intro: You find it waiting — a single envelope, cream-coloured, your name written across it in my hand. The ink is deliberate. Unhurried.

choices.title: A Length of Black Silk Ribbon
choices.hint: Sheer, personal, impossible to ignore.

card.heading: Tie it around your wrist. Wear it all day.
card.body:
Black silk ribbon, cool and soft.

Tie it around your wrist. Wear it all day — wherever you go, whatever you do. You will not take it off until I say.

No one will know. Only you. Only me.

Every time you feel it, think about why it is there. In the afternoon, waiting in line somewhere, press your thumb against it and feel the day tilt slightly toward something else.
card.rule: Do not take it off. Come back tonight.

choices.title: Something Silk, Impossibly Small
choices.hint: Put it on now. Wear it under everything.

card.heading: Put it on now. Wear it under your clothes all day.
card.body:
A box. Inside: something silk that covers almost nothing.

Put it on now and wear it under your clothes all day. Go about your normal life. I will be thinking about it the entire time.

Meetings, errands, ordinary conversations — the whole time it is there, my idea pressed against your skin, a private fact that belongs only to the two of you.

You will be aware of it constantly.
card.rule: By evening you will be more than ready for whatever comes next.

choices.title: A Delicate Chain Collar
choices.hint: It reads as jewellery to everyone else.

card.heading: Wear this today. It means you are mine.
card.body:
A thin delicate chain with a small crimson stone — something that reads as jewellery to everyone else.

Wear this today. It means you are mine. You already knew that. Now you have something to touch when you remember it.

Throughout the day you will touch it without meaning to — at your desk, in the car, once during a conversation with someone who has no idea. Each time you do, think of me.
card.rule: By the time evening comes the anticipation will have been building for so many hours you'll open the next envelope before I even send it.

evening.label: Day One · Evening
evening.intro: You've been carrying it all day. I knew you would. Now the lights go down.

choices.title: The Bedroom — Your Bed
choices.hint: Lights low. The quiet makes everything louder.

card.heading: Go to the bedroom. Lie back. Stay.
card.body:
The bedroom. Lie back on top of the covers exactly as you are, lights low, phone face down.

The quiet makes everything louder.

You are going to touch yourself — slowly, exactly the way I would — and you are going to get yourself right to the edge. And then you are going to stop.

You do not finish tonight. That is not a suggestion. Go right to the edge and pull yourself back. Hold it there. Then come back and confirm you did it.
card.rule: I want you to go to bed wanting. That feeling is mine. I put it there. Keep it for me.

choices.title: The Living Room Couch
choices.hint: Where I sit when I watch you.

card.heading: The couch, in the dark.
card.body:
The couch, in the dark, where I sit when I watch you across the room and think you don't notice.

You notice.

You are going to touch yourself — slowly, exactly the way I would — and get yourself right to the edge. Then you stop.

You do not finish tonight. Go right to the edge, hold it, then pull yourself back. Come back and confirm you did it.

You go to bed still wanting, exactly as I said. It takes a long time to fall asleep.
card.rule: Good girl. That feeling belongs to me.

choices.title: The Floor, Back Against the Bed
choices.hint: A little exposed. Nowhere to hide from yourself.

card.heading: The floor. Your back against the side of the bed.
card.body:
The floor, your back against the side of the bed, head tilted back. Something about it feels right — a little exposed, nowhere to hide from yourself.

You are going to touch yourself — slowly, exactly the way I would — and get yourself right to the edge. Then stop.

You deny yourself until the frustration is extraordinary. Lie there in the aftermath of almost. Think about tomorrow.

That does not help at all.
card.rule: Come back to the app. Confirm it. Then sleep — thinking about tomorrow.

envelopes.intro: You find it waiting — a single envelope, cream-coloured, your name written across it in my hand. The ink is deliberate. Unhurried.

No one will know. Only you. Only me.

Every time you feel it, think about why it is there. In the afternoon, waiting in line somewhere, press your thumb against it and feel the day tilt slightly toward something else.

choices.hint: Where I sit when I watch you.

card.heading: The couch, in the dark.
card.body:
The couch, in the dark, where I sit when I watch you across the room and think you don't notice.

You notice.

You are going to touch yourself — slowly, exactly the way I would — and get yourself right to the edge. Then you stop.

You do not finish tonight. Go right to the edge, hold it, then pull yourself back. Come back and confirm you did it.

You go to bed still wanting, exactly as I said. It takes a long time to fall asleep.

dayPrelude.heading: Before Day 1 Begins
dayPrelude.body: A brief, beautiful lead-in for this day. Use it to set mood, location, or emotional tension before the first envelope opens.

prologue.label: Day Two · Morning
prologue.intro: You wake to a notification. A delivery. At your door, another vase—this time with flowers so perfect they look unreal. Not flowers you would have chosen. Flowers that say something specific. You know exactly who they're from.

choices.title: Keep Them Visible
choices.hint: A reminder, all day long.

card.heading: Put them on the counter where you see them constantly.
card.body: You find a place where the light hits them perfectly. Every time you walk past, you will remember the message in the choice of bloom, the color, the arrangement. All day, they sit where you can see them. A reminder of what's happening to you.
card.rule: Do not move them until tonight.

choices.title: Hide Them
choices.hint: A secret you carry with you.

card.heading: Put them somewhere private. Keep them hidden.
card.body: You move them to a room no one else goes into. Close the door. All day, you know they're there, and no one else does. The secret of them becomes its own kind of turn-on—knowing that this gift exists only for you, hidden, a private fact that belongs to the two of you.
card.rule: Photograph them before you put them away.

morning.label: Day Two · Morning
morning.intro: Last night I left you wanting. That was deliberate. Today I want you to carry it further.

choices.title: Slowly — You Take Your Time
choices.hint: Build something. Feel every step of it.

card.heading: Today: three times to the edge. None over.
card.body:
Three times today — morning, midday, late afternoon — find a private room, a locked door, ten minutes.

The first time: take a long time. Longer than you need to. You are building something and you know it.

Bring yourself within one breath of finishing each time. Then stop. You will not touch yourself again for at least two hours after.

The ache is the point. The ache is my.
card.rule: Log each time with a single word describing how close you were. I am keeping score.

choices.title: Directly — You Know What Works
choices.hint: Efficient. Clean. Almost its own pleasure.

card.heading: Today: three times to the edge. Clean and precise.
card.body:
Three times today — morning, midday, late afternoon — find a private room.

You know exactly what works. Go straight there each time. Within minutes you will be right at the edge — sharp and clear — then stop. Clean, precise, immediate.

Hold yourself in that moment, breathing, then pull back. It will take more discipline than you expect.

You will not touch yourself for at least two hours between each.
card.rule: The efficiency of it is almost its own kind of pleasure. Report back tonight.

choices.title: The Way I Would — Teasing
choices.hint: Not quite what you want. Slightly to the side of it.

card.heading: Today: tease yourself the way I would.
card.body:
Three times today — morning, midday, late afternoon — find a private room.

Do it the way I would: not quite what you want, slightly to the side of it, until the wanting becomes unbearable. Then stop entirely.

Deny yourself again and again until you cannot stand it. Then pull back.

The frustration is extraordinary. That is the point.
card.rule: By this evening you will be so ready that the word alone will almost be enough.

evening.label: Day Two · Evening
evening.intro: You kept the rules all day. Tonight I want to hear you.

choices.title: Record Three Minutes — Nothing Visual
choices.hint: Your mouth close. No script.

card.heading: Record three minutes. Send it unlistened-to.
card.body:
Lie back. Lights off if you want them off. Put the phone against the pillow beside your head.

Tell me — out loud, no script — what you would want me to do to you if I walked into the room right now. Not a fantasy. A plan. Specific. In order. Use the words you would be embarrassed to write down.

You may touch yourself while you speak. You may not finish.
card.rule: Send the recording. Do not listen back before sending.

choices.title: Write What You Cannot Say Out Loud
choices.hint: Paper. Pen. Honesty.

card.heading: Write it by hand. Read it aloud. Burn it.
card.body:
Paper. Pen. No phone nearby. Bare, or in what I last asked you to wear.

Write me a single page. Tell me the filthiest thing you have ever thought about me but never said. Spelling does not matter. Handwriting does not matter. Honesty matters.

When you finish, read it once, out loud, to the empty room. Then burn it in the sink. Photograph the ash.
card.rule: The fact that I will never read it is the point. Do not cheat me by typing it up.

envelopes.label: Day Two · Morning
envelopes.intro: You wake to a notification. A delivery. At your door, another vase—this time with flowers so perfect they look unreal. Not flowers you would have chosen. Flowers that say something specific. You know exactly who they're from.

envelopes.label: Day Two · Morning
envelopes.intro: Last night I left you wanting. That was deliberate. Today I want you to carry it further.

envelopes.label: Day Two · Evening
envelopes.intro: You kept the rules all day. Tonight I want to hear you.

choices.hint: Your mouth close. No script.

card.heading: Record three minutes. Send it unlistened-to.
card.body:
Lie back. Lights off if you want them off. Put the phone against the pillow beside your head.

Tell me — out loud, no script — what you would want me to do to you if I walked into the room right now. Not a fantasy. A plan. Specific. In order. Use the words you would be embarrassed to write down.

You may touch yourself while you speak. You may not finish.
card.rule: Send the recording. Do not listen back before sending.

choices.hint: Paper. Pen. Honesty.

When you finish, read it once, out loud, to the empty room. Then burn it in the sink. Photograph the ash.

dayPrelude.heading: Before Day 2 Begins
dayPrelude.body: A brief, beautiful lead-in for this day. Use it to set mood, location, or emotional tension before the first envelope opens.

prologue.label: Day Three · Morning
prologue.intro: You receive an address and a time. La Paloma. 2 PM. "Come alone. Wear nothing under the dress. I've arranged everything." A massage appointment, but not the kind you expected. You arrive at the spa, and the attendant already knows your name.

choices.title: You Are Nervous
choices.hint: But you go through with it.

card.heading: You arrive early. You wait in the lobby.
card.body: Your hands are shaking slightly as you check in. The attendant smiles knowingly—she's been paid to know. You are led to a private room. The table is set. The oils are warming. You undress slowly, aware of every moment, aware that this was orchestrated, aware that someone wanted you here, exactly like this, exactly now.
card.rule: The massage begins. You try to breathe.

choices.title: You Know What This Is
choices.hint: You arrive ready.

card.heading: You walk in like you own the place.
card.body: You've known since you read the message. This isn't about relaxation. You arrive composed, aware of what you're not wearing, aware of why. The attendant takes you directly to the private room. She closes the door behind you. You undress without hesitation. The anticipation is already unbearable.
card.rule: The massage begins. Your skin is already oversensitive.

morning.label: Day Three · Morning
morning.intro: Tonight you finish — but on my terms. At nine o'clock, you set up your phone on a stand and call me on FaceTime.

choices.title: The Vibrator
choices.hint: The one I know about. Tonight I direct exactly how.

card.heading: Nine o'clock. Camera on your face. Follow exactly.
card.body:
At nine o'clock you set up your phone on a stand. You call me on FaceTime.

The camera does not move from your face for the entire call. I do not need to see anything else tonight — your face is the thing I want. The sounds you make. The way your expression changes. The moment your eyes lose focus.

You use the vibrator. I tell you when to start, when to slow down, how to hold it. My voice stays low and continuous in your ear.

You do not finish until I say the word.
card.rule: When it is over, stay on the call. I want to see your face after too.

choices.title: Just Your Hands
choices.hint: Your hands become my hands.

card.heading: Nine o'clock. Camera on your face. Your hands follow my voice.
card.body:
At nine o'clock you set up your phone on a stand. You call me on FaceTime.

The camera does not move from your face for the entire call.

Your hands only — I direct every movement. Where, how much pressure, when to slow down. Your hands become my hands. It is more intimate than you expected.

You do not finish until I say the word.
card.rule: When it is over, stay on the call. I want to see your face after too.

choices.title: Both — I Decides When
choices.hint: I switch without warning. You follow.

card.heading: Nine o'clock. Camera on your face. I switch when I choose.
card.body:
At nine o'clock you set up your phone on a stand. You call me on FaceTime.

The camera does not move from your face for the entire call.

Both — hands and toy. I switch between them whenever I choose, without warning. You follow. You have no idea what comes next and that is entirely the point.

You do not finish until I say the word.
card.rule: When it is over, stay on the call. I want to see your face after too.

evening.label: Day Three · Evening
evening.intro: The call is over. You are quiet together for a moment, breathing. My expression is something you want to keep.

choices.title: Stay on the Call — Just Breathe
choices.hint: My face. The quiet. Your face after.

card.heading: Stay. Don't rush this part.
card.body:
You finished. Your face did something you cannot describe and I saw all of it.

I stay on the call after. You are quiet together for a moment, breathing. My expression is something you want to keep.

"Good girl," I says.

A pause.

"Tomorrow I want all of you. Start thinking about what you'll wear."

Get some rest. Tomorrow starts the moment you open your eyes.
card.rule: Sleep. Tomorrow is a long day — and it starts the moment you get dressed.

choices.title: The Mirror — After
choices.hint: Watch yourself the way I just did.

card.heading: Undress in front of the mirror. Slowly.
card.body:
After the call ends — lights low, one lamp behind you so there is a shadow.

Undress in front of the mirror exactly as you would if I were seated three feet away.

When you are bare, do not look away. Touch your own throat. Your collarbone. The underside of your breast. Keep your eyes on your eyes.

If you look down, start again.

Finish with one hand flat against the mirror.
card.rule: Tell me tomorrow what you thought about when you almost looked away.

envelopes.label: Day Three · Morning
envelopes.intro: You receive an address and a time. La Paloma. 2 PM. "Come alone. Wear nothing under the dress. I've arranged everything." A massage appointment, but not the kind you expected. You arrive at the spa, and the attendant already knows your name.

choices.hint: I switch without warning. You follow.

card.heading: Nine o'clock. Camera on your face. I switch when I choose.
card.body:
At nine o'clock you set up your phone on a stand. You call me on FaceTime.

The camera does not move from your face for the entire call.

Both — hands and toy. I switch between them whenever I choose, without warning. You follow. You have no idea what comes next and that is entirely the point.

You do not finish until I say the word.
card.rule: When it is over, stay on the call. I want to see your face after too.

envelopes.label: Day Three · Evening
envelopes.intro: The call is over. You are quiet together for a moment, breathing. My expression is something you want to keep.

dayPrelude.heading: Before Day 3 Begins
dayPrelude.body: A brief, beautiful lead-in for this day. Use it to set mood, location, or emotional tension before the first envelope opens.

prologue.label: Day Four · Morning
prologue.intro: Today is not about instruction. Today is about touch. You receive a time and a location. Nothing else. When you arrive, I am already waiting.

choices.title: Fifteen Minutes
choices.hint: A tease. The shortest possible time to feel everything.

card.heading: You kiss me for exactly fifteen minutes.
card.body:
The clock starts the moment your mouth touches mine. Fifteen minutes is not long enough. We both know that. The kiss is everything—it builds, pulls back, builds again. Your hands learn the shape of me. Mine map the curve of your neck, your jaw, the soft inside of your wrist.

When the time ends, we are both breathless. You leave wanting more. That's the point.
card.rule: Set a timer. Do not go over. The anticipation is the entire reward.

choices.title: Twenty-Five Minutes
choices.hint: Long enough to forget where we are. Long enough to matter.

card.heading: You kiss me for twenty-five minutes straight.
card.body:
We lose track. The kiss changes shape—tender, then urgent, then slow again. Your fingers find the small of my back. My hand stays in your hair. We break apart only to catch breath, and then we're back, deeper. By minute twenty, your legs would give out if you weren't pressed against the wall.

When we finally stop, neither of us can speak for a moment. You leave with the taste of me still on your mouth. That's going to stay with you all night.
card.rule: Let yourself get completely lost in it. The time is almost irrelevant.

choices.title: Forty Minutes
choices.hint: We lose ourselves. We lose time. We lose sense of anything outside this.

card.heading: Forty minutes of nothing but kissing.
card.body:
We don't keep track anymore. The kiss goes through phases—almost innocent at first, then increasingly urgent, then something else entirely. Your hands shake. Mine trace every inch of your shoulders, your collarbone, the line of your spine.

We're both overwhelmed. We're both past thinking. By the end of forty minutes, you're not sure you can survive waiting another second for what comes next, and you also know you'll have to, and that's going to destroy you in the best possible way.

You leave barely able to walk straight. The next three days are going to be impossible for you.
card.rule: Do not rush. Do not pull away. Stay in it completely.

evening.label: Day Four · Evening
evening.intro: The makeout is over. You're still unsteady. Tonight is rest. Tomorrow the real ending begins.

choices.title: Sleep Alone
choices.hint: Let the anticipation build through the night.

card.heading: Go to bed alone. Think about what's coming.
card.body:
You go home still tasting me. You get into bed still feeling the ghost of my hands on your skin. You lie awake for hours, your body humming, playing and replaying every second of those minutes.

You do not touch yourself. That's not allowed tonight. You just lie there and want. The anticipation is exquisite torture. By morning, you're going to be completely undone.
card.rule: No touching. Just thinking. Let it build.

envelopes.label: Day Four · Morning
envelopes.intro: Today is not about instruction. Today is about touch. You receive a time and a location. Nothing else. When you arrive, I am already waiting.

choices.title: Fifteen Minutes
choices.hint: A tease. The shortest possible time to feel everything.

card.heading: You kiss me for exactly fifteen minutes.

card.heading: Forty minutes of nothing but kissing.
card.body:
We don't keep track anymore. The kiss goes through phases—almost innocent at first, then increasingly urgent, then something else entirely. Your hands shake. Mine trace every inch of your shoulders, your collarbone, the line of your spine.

We're both overwhelmed. We're both past thinking. By the end of forty minutes, you're not sure you can survive waiting another second for what comes next, and you also know you'll have to, and that's going to destroy you in the best possible way.

You leave barely able to walk straight. The next three days are going to be impossible for you.
card.rule: Do not rush. Do not pull away. Stay in it completely.

envelopes.label: Day Five · Morning
envelopes.intro: Today is about seeing yourself the way I see you. Not costume. Not performance. Beauty, chosen carefully, and worn on purpose from the first hour of the day.

choices.title: The Black Dress
choices.hint: Elegant, close-fitting, impossible to forget.

card.heading: Wear the black dress. Heels. Makeup. The whole thing.
card.body:
The black one. The one that changes your posture the moment it is on.

Do all of it properly today: heels, makeup, your hair done, the details you would only bother with if the evening mattered. It does.

I do not want you dressed up as a joke or a dare. I want you to feel devastating. Composed. Beautiful enough that you catch your own reflection and have to look twice.

Wear it all day with intention. Move through ordinary hours already carrying the knowledge of tonight.
card.rule: Do not underplay it. I want the whole version of you.

inputs.label: What detail makes you feel most beautiful
inputs.placeholder: A detail I should notice first

choices.title: The Silk Dress
choices.hint: Soft, expensive-looking, quietly dangerous.

card.heading: Choose the silk one and let it do what it does.
card.body:
The silk dress is for a different kind of confidence.

Heels, makeup, your hair done, perfume if you want it. No corners cut. No pretending this is casual. I want you to feel polished and unmistakably desired before the evening even begins.

The silk should move when you move. The dress should remind you all day that I was thinking about exactly this version of you: elegant, feminine, and impossible to dismiss.

If someone asks why you look so good today, you do not tell them.
card.rule: Take your time getting ready. The getting ready is part of it.

inputs.label: What part of this look feels most like you
inputs.placeholder: A detail I should pay attention to

choices.title: The Dress That Feels Boldest
choices.hint: The one that makes you stand taller the instant it is on.

card.heading: Wear the one that makes you feel dangerous.
card.body:
Not vulgar. Not careless. Beautiful in the way that makes the room change slightly when you walk into it.

Today I want the full effort: heels, makeup, your hair exactly the way you like it when you want to feel unforgettable. Choose the dress that makes you feel boldest and wear it as if you know precisely what it does.

I want you to feel as sexy and beautiful as I already believe you are. The dress is only part of that. The rest is the way you carry yourself in it.

Let the whole day sharpen around that feeling.
card.rule: Every mirror today is part of the game. Do not look away too quickly.

inputs.label: What makes this dress the right one
inputs.placeholder: A sentence for me about why you chose it

envelopes.label: Day Four · Evening
envelopes.intro: The makeout is over. You're still unsteady. Tonight is rest. Tomorrow the real ending begins.

choices.title: Sleep Alone
choices.hint: Let the anticipation build through the night.

card.heading: Go to bed alone. Think about what's coming.
card.body:
You go home still tasting me. You get into bed still feeling the ghost of my hands on your skin. You lie awake for hours, your body humming, playing and replaying every second of those minutes.

You do not touch yourself. That's not allowed tonight. You just lie there and want. The anticipation is exquisite torture. By morning, you're going to be completely undone.
card.rule: No touching. Just thinking. Let it build.

dayPrelude.heading: Before Day 4 Begins
dayPrelude.body: A brief, beautiful lead-in for this day. Use it to set mood, location, or emotional tension before the first envelope opens.

morning.label: Day Five · Morning
morning.intro: Today is about seeing yourself the way I see you. Not costume. Not performance. Beauty, chosen carefully, and worn on purpose from the first hour of the day.

choices.title: The Black Dress
choices.hint: Elegant, close-fitting, impossible to forget.

card.heading: Wear the black dress. Heels. Makeup. The whole thing.
card.body:
The black one. The one that changes your posture the moment it is on.

Do all of it properly today: heels, makeup, your hair done, the details you would only bother with if the evening mattered. It does.

I do not want you dressed up as a joke or a dare. I want you to feel devastating. Composed. Beautiful enough that you catch your own reflection and have to look twice.

Wear it all day with intention. Move through ordinary hours already carrying the knowledge of tonight.
card.rule: Do not underplay it. I want the whole version of you.

inputs.label: What detail makes you feel most beautiful
inputs.placeholder: A detail I should notice first

choices.title: The Silk Dress
choices.hint: Soft, expensive-looking, quietly dangerous.

card.heading: Choose the silk one and let it do what it does.
card.body:
The silk dress is for a different kind of confidence.

Heels, makeup, your hair done, perfume if you want it. No corners cut. No pretending this is casual. I want you to feel polished and unmistakably desired before the evening even begins.

The silk should move when you move. The dress should remind you all day that I was thinking about exactly this version of you: elegant, feminine, and impossible to dismiss.

If someone asks why you look so good today, you do not tell them.
card.rule: Take your time getting ready. The getting ready is part of it.

inputs.label: What part of this look feels most like you
inputs.placeholder: A detail I should pay attention to

choices.title: The Dress That Feels Boldest
choices.hint: The one that makes you stand taller the instant it is on.

card.heading: Wear the one that makes you feel dangerous.
card.body:

Let the whole day sharpen around that feeling.
card.rule: Every mirror today is part of the game. Do not look away too quickly.

inputs.label: What makes this dress the right one
inputs.placeholder: A sentence for me about why you chose it

evening.label: Day Five · Evening
evening.intro: Tonight is the last decision before the ending. One path means you surrender the shape of tomorrow. The other means you tell me exactly how you want it built.
evening.choicesIntro: Pick honestly. The next day appears based on this choice alone.

choices.title: I Choose
choices.hint: You hand me the final shape of it.

card.heading: Tomorrow belongs to me.
card.body:
You are done arranging the ending. I will decide the room, the pacing, the atmosphere, the first instruction, and the last one.

Your only task is to arrive ready. Private. Present. Uncomplicated.

Tomorrow, do not negotiate with the mood. Step into it.
card.rule: Sleep well. Keep tomorrow clear. I will tell you when to begin.

realText.message: {{herName}}, tomorrow is mine. Keep your evening clear and keep the room ready.

choices.title: She Chooses
choices.hint: You set the details. I make them real.

card.heading: Tomorrow will be built around what you choose.
card.body:
If you want the last day to reflect you fully, this is the moment to say so.

Tomorrow you will be asked for specifics: the room, the mood, the presentation, the level of structure, whether toys belong in the room at all, whether the atmosphere should feel tender, exacting, playful, or overwhelming.

I will not mock honesty. I will use it.
card.rule: Tomorrow, answer clearly. Give me something real to build from.

realText.message: {{herName}}, tomorrow is yours to shape. Come ready with real answers.

envelopes.label: Day Five · Evening
envelopes.intro: Tonight is the last decision before the ending. One path means you surrender the shape of tomorrow. The other means you tell me exactly how you want it built.
envelopes.choicesIntro: Pick honestly. The next day appears based on this choice alone.

choices.title: I Choose
choices.hint: You hand me the final shape of it.

card.heading: Tomorrow belongs to me.
card.body:
You are done arranging the ending. I will decide the room, the pacing, the atmosphere, the first instruction, and the last one.

Your only task is to arrive ready. Private. Present. Uncomplicated.

Tomorrow, do not negotiate with the mood. Step into it.
card.rule: Sleep well. Keep tomorrow clear. I will tell you when to begin.

realText.message: {{herName}}, tomorrow is mine. Keep your evening clear and keep the room ready.

choices.title: She Chooses
choices.hint: You set the details. I make them real.

card.heading: Tomorrow will be built around what you choose.
card.body:
If you want the last day to reflect you fully, this is the moment to say so.

Tomorrow you will be asked for specifics: the room, the mood, the presentation, the level of structure, whether toys belong in the room at all, whether the atmosphere should feel tender, exacting, playful, or overwhelming.

I will not mock honesty. I will use it.
card.rule: Tomorrow, answer clearly. Give me something real to build from.

realText.message: {{herName}}, tomorrow is yours to shape. Come ready with real answers.

envelopes.label: Day Six · Morning
envelopes.intro: I chose. The day feels different because there is nothing left for you to design, only to answer.

choices.title: I Follow The Plan
choices.hint: No edits. No softening. No substitutions.

card.heading: Prepare exactly enough. Not more.
card.body:
Choose the room I know suits you best. Put it in order. Keep the light low. Lay out only what belongs there.

Dress the way I would expect when I want you attentive rather than ornamental.

At some point today, stand still for one full minute and think only this: tonight is already moving toward you.
card.rule: By evening, you are not improvising. You are arriving.

realText.message: {{herName}}, tonight you follow. Be ready when I tell you to begin.

envelopes.label: Day Six · Evening
envelopes.intro: The final envelope does not ask what you want. It tells you where to be, how to hold yourself, and what kind of night this will become.
envelopes.choicesIntro: There is only one path here. The choice was yesterday.

choices.title: Follow Exactly
choices.hint: The authored ending. My hand on the structure of it.

card.heading: Tonight, you let the plan close around you.
card.body:
You enter the room already aware of me. The details are not accidental. The light, the music, the pace, the distance between one instruction and the next — all of it was chosen with care.

You are not being rushed. You are being placed.

What matters most is not one specific act, but the fact that neither of you is pretending anymore. The story is no longer rehearsal. It is recognition.

Later, in the quiet that follows, I tell you exactly which version of you I wanted tonight — and why it was this one.
card.rule: Keep the room exactly as it is for a little while longer.

envelopes.label: Day Six · Morning
envelopes.intro: You asked to choose the last night yourself. This morning is where you answer honestly enough for me to build the rest.

choices.title: I Choose The Shape Of Tonight
choices.hint: Private. Specific. Entirely yours.

card.heading: Build the atmosphere. I will make it real.
card.body:
Choose clearly. The room. The atmosphere. The way you want to be seen. Whether tonight should feel tender, structured, playful, exacting, or cinematic.

If there is anything that belongs in the room, say so. If there is anything that does not, say that too.

This is not about being bold for the sake of it. It is about being accurate.
card.rule: By evening, you do not want ambiguity. You want the right night.

inputs.label: What should the ending feel like
inputs.placeholder: Describe the ending in a few words

realText.message: {{herName}}, send me the truth when the app asks. I am building tonight around it.

envelopes.label: Day Six · Evening
envelopes.intro: Tonight reflects what you chose this morning. The room is no longer generic. The mood is no longer accidental. It feels built for you because it was.
envelopes.choicesIntro: Pick the tone that comes closest to what you asked me to make real.

dayPrelude.heading: Before Day 5 Begins
dayPrelude.body: A brief, beautiful lead-in for this day. Use it to set mood, location, or emotional tension before the first envelope opens.
